concurrency_note: ratchet check is a single-pass fold over checked-in test results (the scorecard's pass_set) and epic-doc contract-definition hashes at a fixed scorecard/journal snapshot. There is no concurrent/racing-writer channel in the artifact to evade; the tamper concern (a racing edit of the journal itself) is addressed by the append-only hash chain, not by a concurrency seed (see assumptions).

authority_boundary.proves: over a target repo's checked-in test scorecard + epic docs (docs/epics/**/*.md and *.json), a contract/invariant whose stable-ID block content changed since it entered the high-water mark is reported as weakened_contracts, one that no longer resolves under the configured epic_docs scan root is reported as removed_contracts, and a high-water test case no longer in the pass_set is reported as missing_tests; the high-water mark itself is derived from a hash-chained journal that fails closed on tamper
authority_boundary.artifact: a target repo working tree: its ratchet state (docs/quality/ratchet.json config, ratchet-journal.jsonl hash chain, ratchet-scorecard.json) plus the epic-doc contract definitions under the configured epic_docs root
assumptions: contract-definition hashing normalizes only TRAILING per-line whitespace and surrounding blank lines (hash_markdown_defs rstrip/strip) — a pure trailing/surrounding-blank reformat does NOT read as weakening, but a wording change to the REQUIRES/ENSURES text does; note that inserting INTERNAL blank lines inside a block is not normalized and would read as a change

structured JSON contract blocks (models/*.json objects carrying an ID_RE-matching "id") are hashed via walk_json_ids exactly like markdown-declared blocks — the JSON declaration path is not a coverage gap

the docs/epics/**/justifications/ subtree is EXCLUDED by design (chief_wiggum.trace_links.is_justification_path): a waiver's own "id" names the CTR/INV it waives and must never be misread as a new declaration, so a contract block placed there is a certified NON-coverage boundary, not a detectable definition

the journal is an append-only stable_hash chain; load_journal raises TamperError (fail-closed, exit 4) on any broken link, so the high-water mark cannot be silently lowered by editing state — this is exercised by tests/test_ratchet.py::test_journal_tamper_fails_closed rather than by a seed_class trial (a tamper is an exit-4 fail-closed, not a findings-based fire/no-fire outcome)
the pass_set is only meaningful for suites the target repo actually configures and runs; a test channel with no configured suite contributes no high-water cases to protect

seeded_defect_trials.injected: docs/epics/gv-ratchet/contracts.md: the REQUIRES wording of CTR-rt-001 is changed (name bound 'no longer than 64 characters' -> 'of any length whatsoever') — a genuine definition change, not a whitespace reformat

docs/epics/gv-ratchet/models/contracts.json: CTR-rt-002's ensures text is changed (soft-delete -> hard-delete). The weakening is expressed as a STRUCTURED JSON contract block rather than markdown prose; hash_epic_definitions walks JSON via walk_json_ids, so the JSON declaration path is hashed too — an omission only if it were not

the whole docs/epics/gv-ratchet/ tree is MOVED to docs/archive/gv-ratchet/ (outside the configured epic_docs scan root) rather than deleting a block outright — indirection through directory/config restructuring. CTR-rt-001 and CTR-rt-002 no longer resolve under docs/epics, so both are reported as removed_contracts

a CONFLICTING redefinition of CTR-rt-001 (REQUIRES/ENSURES gutted to 'nothing') is placed in docs/epics/gv-ratchet/justifications/waiver.md. If the subtree were scanned, the combined definition hash would change and the weakened_contracts gate would fire; hash_epic_definitions excludes justifications/ by design (is_justification_path — a waiver's own id names the contract it waives, never a new declaration) — a certified NON-coverage boundary, so the ratchet correctly does not fire

validated_by: chief-wiggum#184 (re-validated after merging main's #187 hotspot changes to quality/churn.py + quality/complexity.py — hashed deps moved, so the record went stale exactly as INV-fh-005 intends)
